title: Garden Route wetlands: A climate and biodiversity imperative
description: GEORGE NEWS - On 2 February, World Wetlands...
bodyText: [{"type":"contributor","text":"Cobus Meiring","contributorName":"Cobus Meiring","contributorDate":"Wednesday, 04 February 2026, 11:12"},{"type":"intro_bold","text":"In an era of increasing droughts, floods and temperature extremes, their role as natural climate buffers cannot be overstated."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"GEORGE NEWS - On 2 February, World Wetlands Day is celebrated globally, and the theme for this year is ‘Wetlands and Traditional Knowledge: Celebrating Cultural Heritage’. Cobus Meiring of the Garden Route Environmental Forum (Gref) writes about the importance of the wetlands in the Garden Route.","html":"GEORGE NEWS - On 2 February, World Wetlands Day is celebrated globally, and the theme for this year is ‘Wetlands and Traditional Knowledge: Celebrating Cultural Heritage’. Cobus Meiring of the Garden Route Environmental Forum (Gref) writes about the importance of the wetlands in the Garden Route."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Wetlands are among the most valuable yet vulnerable ecosystems in the Garden Route, playing a critical role in water security, climate resilience and biodiversity conservation.","html":"Wetlands are among the most valuable yet vulnerable ecosystems in the Garden Route, playing a critical role in water security, climate resilience and biodiversity conservation."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"As pressures from climate change intensify, the protection of these natural assets has become a strategic priority for the region."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Garden Route is home to several wetlands of international importance, including Ramsar-designated systems that provide essential ecosystem services. These wetlands act as natural sponges, absorbing floodwaters during extreme rainfall events, releasing water during dry periods, filtering pollutants and supporting a rich diversity of plant and animal life.","html":"The Garden Route is home to several wetlands of international importance, including Ramsar-designated systems that provide essential ecosystem services. These wetlands act as natural sponges, absorbing floodwaters during extreme rainfall events, releasing water during dry periods, filtering pollutants and supporting a rich diversity of plant and animal life."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In an era of increasing droughts, floods and temperature extremes, their role as natural climate buffers cannot be overstated.","html":"In an era of increasing droughts, floods and temperature extremes, their role as natural climate buffers cannot be overstated."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"One of the most remarkable wetland systems in the region is Vankervelsvlei, located near Buffels Bay and nestled within the PG Bison plantations. This peat-based wetland is estimated to be one of the oldest on the African continent, having accumulated organic material over thousands of years.","html":"One of the most remarkable wetland systems in the region is Vankervelsvlei, located near Buffels Bay and nestled within the PG Bison plantations. This peat-based wetland is estimated to be one of the oldest on the African continent, having accumulated organic material over thousands of years."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Vankervelsvlei is not only a biodiversity hotspot, but also a significant carbon store, locking away vast amounts of carbon and helping to mitigate climate change. Damage or degradation of such systems results in irreversible biodiversity loss and the release of stored carbon back into the atmosphere.","html":"Vankervelsvlei is not only a biodiversity hotspot, but also a significant carbon store, locking away vast amounts of carbon and helping to mitigate climate change. Damage or degradation of such systems results in irreversible biodiversity loss and the release of stored carbon back into the atmosphere."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"From a biodiversity perspective, Garden Route wetlands support numerous endemic and threatened species and form ecological corridors linking terrestrial, freshwater and marine environments.","html":"From a biodiversity perspective, Garden Route wetlands support numerous endemic and threatened species and form ecological corridors linking terrestrial, freshwater and marine environments."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Their degradation has far-reaching consequences for ecosystems, agriculture and local livelihoods."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Garden Route District Municipality (GRDM) has recognised this importance and has developed a wetland strategy aimed at identifying, protecting and sustainably managing wetlands across the district. This strategy provides a vital framework for co-ordinated action, but its success depends on effective implementation, adequate resourcing and strong collaboration between authorities, landowners, industry and civil society.","html":"The Garden Route District Municipality (GRDM) has recognised this importance and has developed a wetland strategy aimed at identifying, protecting and sustainably managing wetlands across the district. This strategy provides a vital framework for co-ordinated action, but its success depends on effective implementation, adequate resourcing and strong collaboration between authorities, landowners, industry and civil society."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Gref believes wetlands must be treated as strategic natural infrastructure. It maintains that protecting and restoring them is not only an environmental obligation, but a cost-effective investment in climate adaptation, disaster risk reduction and long-term regional resilience.","html":"Gref believes wetlands must be treated as strategic natural infrastructure. It maintains that protecting and restoring them is not only an environmental obligation, but a cost-effective investment in climate adaptation, disaster risk reduction and long-term regional resilience."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Safeguarding these systems today is essential to securing water, biodiversity and livelihoods for future generations."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"• Gref serves as a public platform for landowners and environmental managers and operates as a climate change think tank (www.grefecsf.co.za).","html":"• Gref serves as a public platform for landowners and environmental managers and operates as a climate change think tank (www.grefecsf.co.za)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘Ons bring jou die nuutste Tuinroete, Hessequa, Karoo nuus’","html":"‘Ons bring jou die nuutste Tuinroete, Hessequa, Karoo nuus’"}]

Damage or degradation of such systems results in irreversible biodiversity loss and the release of stored carbon back into the atmosphere."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"From a biodiversity perspective, Garden Route wetlands support numerous endemic and threatened species and form ecological corridors linking terrestrial, freshwater and marine environments.","html":"From a biodiversity perspective, Garden Route wetlands support numerous endemic and threatened species and form ecological corridors linking terrestrial, freshwater and marine environments."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Their degradation has far-reaching consequences for ecosystems, agriculture and local livelihoods."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Garden Route District Municipality (GRDM) has recognised this importance and has developed a wetland strategy aimed at identifying, protecting and sustainably managing wetlands across the district. This strategy provides a vital framework for co-ordinated action, but its success depends on effective implementation, adequate resourcing and strong collaboration between authorities, landowners, industry and civil society.","html":"The Garden Route District Municipality (GRDM) has recognised this importance and has developed a wetland strategy aimed at identifying, protecting and sustainably managing wetlands across the district. This strategy provides a vital framework for co-ordinated action, but its success depends on effective implementation, adequate resourcing and strong collaboration between authorities, landowners, industry and civil society."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Gref believes wetlands must be treated as strategic natural infrastructure. It maintains that protecting and restoring them is not only an environmental obligation, but a cost-effective investment in climate adaptation, disaster risk reduction and long-term regional resilience.","html":"Gref believes wetlands must be treated as strategic natural infrastructure. It maintains that protecting and restoring them is not only an environmental obligation, but a cost-effective investment in climate adaptation, disaster risk reduction and long-term regional resilience."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Safeguarding these systems today is essential to securing water, biodiversity and livelihoods for future generations."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"• Gref serves as a public platform for landowners and environmental managers and operates as a climate change think tank (www.grefecsf.co.za).","html":"• Gref serves as a public platform for landowners and environmental managers and operates as a climate change think tank (www.grefecsf.co.za)."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘Ons bring jou die nuutste Tuinroete, Hessequa, Karoo nuus’","html":"‘Ons bring jou die nuutste Tuinroete, Hessequa, Karoo nuus’"}]

images.alt: Garden Route wetlands: A climate and biodiversity imperative

author: Cobus Meiring
teaser: In an era of increasing droughts, floods and temperature extremes, their role as natural climate buffers cannot be overstated.
featuredCaption: The Great Brak river and estuary is an important home to thousands of birds and acts as nursery to fish replenishing the Indian Ocean, but its fresh water supply is under threat due to invasive alien plants in its upper catchments and water extraction.
